Title: Significant enhancement of fatty acid composition in seeds of the allohexaploid, Camelina sativa, using CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal omega-6 fatty acid desaturase; membrane-bound desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Seed fatty acid desaturation / unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid to polyunsaturated fatty acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Linoleic acid (18:2); indirectly supports linolenic acid (18:3) accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into oleic acid to form linoleic acid; this promotes downstream formation of linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of FAD2 markedly increases seed oleic acid and total monounsaturated fatty acids while reducing polyunsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; Sanger sequencing; Illumina Amplicon-Seq; fatty acid profiling by gas chromatography; inheritance analysis in later generations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD2 was the primary experimental target for CRISPR/Cas9 editing to improve seed oil quality. 2. Disruption of FAD2 increased oleic acid from ~16% to >50% in Camelina seeds and to ~55–60% in selected Arabidopsis seed lines. 3. Increased oleic acid was accompanied by strong decreases in linoleic acid and linolenic acid. 4. All three Camelina homoeologous FAD2 gene pairs in the A, B and C subgenomes were efficiently edited. 5. Both germ-line and somatic mutations contributed to the altered seed fatty acid phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves nutritional quality and oxidative stability of seed oil; enhances suitability for specialty chemicals and biofuel production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All three pairs of Camelina FAD2 genes were reported to be active in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Illumina Amplicon-MiSeq DNA sequencing; Sanger sequencing; fatty acid metabolite profiling by gas chromatography
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for Cas9 (constitutive); Arabidopsis U6 promoter for sgRNA (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Seed-to-seed variability due to somatic mosaicism during seed development; stable fixation in allohexaploid Camelina required multiple generations of editing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering high-oleic Camelina and Arabidopsis seed oil; stable elite lines will likely require fixation of multiple homoeolog knockouts and removal of editing transgenes; authors propose stacking with FAE1 knockout for further oleic acid enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FAD2 homodimer; possible nonfunctional heterodimer formation with mutant FAD2 polypeptides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Camelina, the high-oleic phenotype likely reflects combined effects of inherited germ-line knockouts and somatic editing in developing seeds. The paper also discusses possible dominant-negative inhibition ('subunit poisoning') by truncated FAD2 proteins. Off-target mutations were not detected at tested candidate loci.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes elongation of fatty acyl-CoA substrates to produce longer-chain fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux away from oleic acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Authors propose that future knockout of FAE1 genes should further boost oleic acid content in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAE1 was not experimentally manipulated in this study. 2. It was identified in the conclusion as a future editing target expected to further increase seed oleic acid in Camelina. 3. No direct functional validation was provided in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves nutritional quality and industrial value of seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggested future gene-stacking/editing target with FAD2 knockout to obtain even higher oleic acid Camelina oil
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned only as a proposed future target; not a primary experimental gene in the Results.
